title: Paranomus micrantnus
intro:

The genus Paranomus is a group of flowering plants in the family Proteaceae, commonly known as the South African proteas. 
Paranomus plants are known for their unique, long-lasting flowers and are often used in floral arrangements.
taxonomy: Paranomus micrantnus is a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. It is a shrub that is endemic to the Western Cape province in South Africa. The plant is classified as being in the genus Paranomus and the subfamily Proteoideae. Paranomus micrantnus is further classified as being in the family Proteaceae, which is a family of flowering plants that are native to the southern hemisphere.
